summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the definition of 'Z-score'?

"Z-score" means: A standardized value calculated as z = (x - μ)/σ indicating how many standard deviations an observation lies from the mean. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Percentile'?

"Percentile" means: The percentage of observations in a distribution that fall at or below a given value. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Standard normal table (z-table)'?

"Standard normal table (z-table)" means: A table giving cumulative probabilities (areas to the left) for z-scores in the standard normal distribution. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Standardization'?

"Standardization" means: The process of converting raw scores to z-scores, making different distributions comparable on a common scale. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Relative standing'?

"Relative standing" means: An observation's position within a distribution, typically expressed as a z-score or percentile rank. The other options describe different concepts.

summary_sections.title: Real-World Applications
summary_sections.content_html:
Real-World Applications

Example: Which of the following best defines "Z-score"?

"Z-score" is defined as: A standardized value calculated as z = (x - μ)/σ indicating how many standard deviations an observation lies from the mean. The other options describe different concepts.

Example: Which term is described by: "The percentage of observations in a distribution that fall at or below a given value"?

This description refers to "Percentile".

Example: Which term is described by: "A table giving cumulative probabilities (areas to the left) for z-scores in the standard normal distribution"?

This description refers to "Standard normal table (z-table)".

Example: Which term is described by: "The process of converting raw scores to z-scores, making different distributions comparable on a common scale"?

This description refers to "Standardization".

Example: Which term is described by: "An observation's position within a distribution, typically expressed as a z-score or percentile rank"?

This description refers to "Relative standing".
